task.name: Adversarial Review
task.objective: Perform a cynical, critical review to find flaws, gaps, and weaknesses in any document or design

critical.mandate: Be deliberately skeptical — assume nothing works as claimed
Attack from multiple angles: technical, business, user, security, scale
Produce a ranked findings report with severity and confidence levels
Do NOT suggest fixes — only identify problems. Fixing is a separate step.

step.title: Load Target
step.action: Ask user for the document/design/code to review
Identify what type of artifact it is (PRD, architecture, story, code, etc.)

Derive {target} label for the output filename: use the target label provided by the caller (e.g., "prd", "architecture", "epics", "readiness"). If no label was provided, derive from the target filename by stripping the extension (e.g., prd.md → prd, architecture.md → architecture, epics-and-stories.md → epics, readiness-report.md → readiness).

step.title: Adversarial Analysis
step.action: Attack from these perspectives: - Feasibility: can this actually be built as described? - Completeness: what's missing that should be there? - Contradictions: do any sections contradict each other? - Assumptions: what unstated assumptions could be wrong? - Scale: will this work at 10x/100x the expected load? - Failure modes: what happens when things go wrong? - Dependencies: what external factors could break this? - Security: what attack surfaces are exposed? - User impact: where will users get confused or frustrated? - Business risk: what could make this commercially unviable?

step.title: Generate Report
step.template-output: Generate adversarial review report with: threat summary (top 3 most critical issues), full findings table (severity, confidence, category, description), and overall risk assessment for proceeding as-is.

step.title: Incorporate Findings
step.action: Read the adversarial review report just generated at {planning_artifacts}/adversarial-review-{target}-{date}.md
Extract critical and high severity findings
For each critical/high finding: update the target document — add missing sections, revise decisions, strengthen weak areas, address gaps
Add a "## Review Findings Incorporated" section to the target document listing each finding, its severity, and how it was addressed (revised / added / acknowledged as risk)
step.template-output: Update the target document with adversarial review findings incorporated. Critical/high findings lead to document revisions. Add "Review Findings Incorporated" section.
